mentions.note: Leading market rally alongside Chevron on Iran geopolitical risk; problematic leadership—energy costs are tax on economy, means everyone else losing when oils win.
Throws off massive cash; reports Friday alongside Chevron.
Oil stock down 1-2% signals war winds down, Gulf reopens, crude peaks. Oil market typically sees all; price action suggests normalized geopolitical environment ahead.
Friday PCE data unpredictable but should go for bulls; energy play benefits from elevated crude backdrop.
If oil soars to $120+ due to Iran war escalation, even Exxon would get crushed as part of broad S&P 500 selloff. Wait for war resolution before buying energy.
Oil stocks surged on 82nd Airborne Middle East deployment signal; benefiting from geopolitical risk premium despite president's peace rhetoric.
Caller held at ~160; Cramer prefers Chevron but says don't cash out to switch. Stay in Exxon; oil up 34% YTD essential ownership. Cramer regrets selling oil for charitable trust; clear mistake.

Caller Jim (CA) compares to Chevron; Cramer says Exxon fine but Chevron superior operator under Wirth; sticking with Chevron thesis.
